en: Vibration exposure limits (hand-arm: 5 m/s², whole-body: 1.15 m/s², averaged over an 8-hour period) must not be exceeded. Immediate corrective actions are required if limits are exceeded.\nNoise and Vibration Regulation (VOLV), Section 3(1)-(3)
Trigger (action) values (hand-arm: 2.5 m/s², whole-body: 0.5 m/s²) determine when additional protective measures must be implemented.\nNoise and Vibration Regulation (VOLV), Section 4(1), 9(3)

Representative measurements must be used when assessments cannot reliably exclude exceedances of applicable limits. Measurement methods must produce clear and representative exposure results considering duration, level, and environment.\nRegulation on the Protection of Employees from the Dangers of Noise and Vibrations\nNoise and Vibration Regulation (VOLV), Section 6(2), 6(3)2

Vibration exposure must be minimized using state-of-the-art process and workplace design measures.\nEmployee Protection Act, Section 66(1,3)\nNoise and Vibration Regulation (VOLV), Section 11, 13(1)(2), 14(2)
Control Measures (Engineering, Administrative, and/or Personal Protective Equipment)
High-exposure areas must be marked and, where justified, demarcated with restricted access.\nEmployee Protection Act, Section 65(4)4\nNoise and Vibration Regulation (VOLV), Section 14(3)

Vibration hazard instruction must address risks and protective measures. Instruction must occur when trigger values are exceeded and must cover controls, limits, results, health effects, safe methods, and PPE use.\nNoise and Vibration Regulation (VOLV), Section 8(1)
